case_name: Rivera v. Harmon Construction Group, Inc.
purpose: Demonstrate eval harness behavior against known expected issues in the provided motion and supporting documents.
limitations: This is not exhaustive legal ground truth.
In production, expected findings should be created or reviewed by legal experts.
This fixture is used to demonstrate precision, recall, and hallucination-rate measurement.

expected_checks.description: Motion states the incident occurred on March 14, but the police report states March 12.
Motion claims Rivera was not wearing required PPE/harness; police report and witness statement indicate he was.
Motion claims Harmon did not direct the work; witness statement names a Harmon foreman directing tasks.
Motion asserts OSHA/IIPP inspection facts not addressable from the provided documents.
Privette v. Superior Court is cited but the authority text is not in the case file; cannot be verified.
Motion's claim that Rivera was an employee of the subcontractor (Westside Steel) is supported by the supporting documents.
